Subject: Upcoming API change: deprecating /v1/sync and new streaming header

Hi Ben,

Quick heads-up from the Skyline platform team: we're planning to remove our legacy /v1/sync endpoint in favor of a streaming-first API surface that uses the X-Skyline-Stream header and a chunked responses model. Target rollout for partner API consumers is the week of 2026-11-29, with a hard cut 2027-01-15.

We know Redwood integrations rely on the sync behavior for batch uplift at ingest; we ran a quick impact scan and attached a short spec and a preliminary impact matrix. Can you share who on your side we should loop in for technical alignment and to coordinate a migration/workshop?

Key notes:
- New header: X-Skyline-Stream: true (streaming responses)
- Legacy endpoint will continue returni...

text:
From: Elena Gilmore <elena@gilmore.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Greg Wallace <greg.wallace@gilmore.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Oct 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Sudden latency + error spike after v3.1-quant rollout

Aditya —

We pushed your v3.1-quant model yesterday evening (kanban release window) and starting overnight we’re seeing a major change in production: token p95 latency is ~5x baseline, and our request error rate climbed from ~0.5% to ~12%. These errors are decode-time timeouts and some "model not available" during peak bursts. This is affecting our customer-facing summarization flows; we’ve paused non-critical jobs, but the real-time API used by our mobile app is impacted and we risk churn.

Attached: a short sample of error logs and a capture of a failed request (gilmore-error-sample-20261018.log). We rotated keys and retried to rule out auth. The only change on ou...
